prompt:
The checkpoint package (job progress snapshots for our batch jobs) does its job in prod but is a wall for testing: os.MkdirAll/WriteFile/ReadDir/Remove and time.Now() are called inline all over, so the importer team tests it with real sleeps and a real spool dir, and that suite flaked twice last week on a slow runner. Ticket: dependency-inject the seams, keep the behavior.

Target shape: two small interfaces in the package — Clock with Now() time.Time, and Store with Write(name string, data []byte) error, Read(name string) ([]byte, error), List() ([]string, error), Remove(name string) error (names only, no paths — the store owns location). A Keeper built with New(store Store, clock Clock) carries the current functions as methods: Save(job, payload) (name, error), Latest(job) ([]byte, time.Time, error), Resume(job, maxAge) ([]byte, bool, error), SweepStale(maxAge) ([]string, error). Production keeps working through shipped adapters: NewOSStore(dir) (creates the directory, lists/reads/writes/removes real files, skips subdirectories) and SystemClock{} for the wall clock. The old package-level Save/Latest/Resume/SweepStale go away — callers construct a Keeper now. ErrNoCheckpoint stays.

Nothing observable changes: same <job>-<10-digit-unix>.ckpt names (ops sorts these lexically, keep the zero padding), job parsed from the LAST hyphen so hyphenated job names keep working, newest-by-embedded-timestamp selection regardless of listing order, Resume treating age of exactly maxAge as still fresh and missing/stale as (nil, false, nil), SweepStale deleting strictly-older checkpoints across all jobs and returning removed names ascending while leaving non-checkpoint files alone. keeper_test.go is the contract — it drives everything with a scripted clock and an in-memory store and also exercises the OS adapters; go test -race -timeout 30s ./... must pass without touching the test file.
